title: Weighted Mazes, Dijkstra, Heaps, and A* · Behavior Case: Astar Heuristic
summary: The heuristic overestimates, heap priorities are mutated in place, stale entries finalize wrong costs, and geometric distance ignores movement rules. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the astar heuristic increment in weighted Dijkstra and A-star comparison engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose and scale an admissible, consistent heuristic for the actual movement and cost model.
Push improved priorities and reject stale heap entries by comparing popped and recorded costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-astar-heuristic — Choose and scale an admissible, consistent heuristic for the actual movement and cost model.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-weighted-dijkstra-astar-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-weighted-dijkstra-astar-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_weighted_dijkstra_astar_5(reported_cost: int = 27, oracle_cost: int = 27, expansions: int = 34, stale_entries_skipped: int = 6, heuristic_admissible: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if reported_cost < 0 or oracle_cost < 0 or expansions < 1:
        return False, reported_cost, "weighted-count"
    if reported_cost != oracle_cost:
        return False, reported_cost, "nonoptimal-cost"
    if stale_entries_skipped < 0:
        return False, stale_entries_skipped, "heap-accounting"
    if not heuristic_admissible:
        return False, expansions, "heuristic-overestimate"
    assert reported_cost == oracle_cost
    return True, reported_cost, "optimal-weighted-route"

# Evidence: maze-astar-heuristic-pqkbfi
# Competency: maze-astar-heuristic.
# Case 1490: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_astar_heuristic_pqkbfi(reported_cost: int = 27, oracle_cost: int = 27, expansions: int = 34, stale_entries_skipped: int = 6, heuristic_admissible: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pqkbfi = "pqkbfi-ifbkqp-1490"
    assert evidence_variant_pqkbfi
    evidence_axes_pqkbfi = (
        "gridpqkbfi",
        "topology1490",
        "searchifbkqp",
        "eventpqkbfi1490",
        "access1490pqkbfi",
        "recoveryifbkqp1490",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pqkbfi)) == 6
    if reported_cost < 0 or oracle_cost < 0 or expansions < 1:
        return False, reported_cost, "weighted-count"
    if reported_cost != oracle_cost:
        return False, reported_cost, "nonoptimal-cost"
    if stale_entries_skipped < 0:
        return False, stale_entries_skipped, "heap-accounting"
    if not heuristic_admissible:
        return False, expansions, "heuristic-overestimate"
    assert reported_cost == oracle_cost
    return True, reported_cost, "optimal-weighted-route"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose and scale an admissible, consistent heuristic for the actual movement and cost model.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Astar Heuristic model · Theory Astar Heuristic, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Astar Heuristic precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Astar Heuristic model · Theory Astar Heuristic, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 6943 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate weighted relaxation, heap staleness, heuristic limits, route cost, and optimality; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Astar Heuristic model · Theory Astar Heuristic, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Manhattan distance is valid regardless of diagonal moves or minimum edge cost.
